hero_sub: Board-certified internal medicine meets advanced functional testing — to uncover the root causes behind complex, chronic illness and guide your return to real vitality.
bio1: Dr. Kanani began at the University of California, Berkeley, earning a degree in Molecular Biology and Neuroscience, then studied medicine at the Albert Einstein College of Medicine in New York, earning his doctorate in 2007. He completed a three-year residency in Internal Medicine at Montefiore Medical Center in the Bronx — an academic center focused on care for the underserved.
bio2: Returning to Northern California, he worked in primary care and as a hospitalist before realizing he could help people heal from chronic illness far more effectively with a holistic approach. He began studying Functional Medicine through the Institute for Functional Medicine and spent two years working under Dr. Isaac Eliaz, a pioneer in Integrative Oncology. From 2017 to 2022 he practiced at the Sutter Institute for Health & Healing alongside an interdisciplinary team of acupuncturists, chiropractors, health coaches, and nutritionists.
bio3: Today, in private practice, he offers a wider array of services — combining functional-medicine testing, IV nutritional therapies, and his conventional training into an approach that is truly integrative, built around each patient's genetics, needs, lifestyle, and story.
hours: By appointment — please confirm the practice's hours.
contact_intro: See whether this approach is the right fit for you — no cost, no pressure. You can also call the office directly.
